ai_insights.executive_summary: The LOA network posted a composite Performance Score of 22.75 out of 100 (where 50 represents the network average), reflecting persistent operational headwinds across all four clinics. While scheduler compliance and iAssign adoption remain solid, patient delays and chair utilization patterns reveal structural scheduling and workflow challenges that warrant targeted intervention over the next quarter.
highlights: Summerville delivered the month's strongest turnaround, with scheduler compliance climbing 31.4 percentage points from 46.7% in January to 61.3% in February, alongside a 9.2% gain in chair utilization to 81.1%. NorthCharleston's average patient delay has improved steadily over three consecutive months, reaching 23.9 minutes in February (3-month average of 25.3 minutes), which now sits below the company average of 26.39 minutes and suggests sustainable progress in patient flow discipline.
concerns: We noticed that WestAshely's average patient delay stands at 35.3 minutes, a gap of 34% above the company average of 26.39 minutes—the largest single performance gap across the network. At the same time, WestAshely's chair utilization of 60.5% remains the lowest in the LOA group and substantially trails the company benchmark of 70.19%, suggesting that scheduling density and template alignment may both merit review. Additionally, NorthCharleston's treatments running past closing time average 2.8 per day (1.6 standard deviations above the group mean of 1.2), which has grown alongside improved delay metrics—worth exploring whether scheduling discipline has shifted end-of-day workload upstream rather than eliminating it.
recommendations: We recommend that WestAshely's leadership team conduct a focused audit of its scheduling templates against iOptimize recommendations, with particular attention to the appointment density and sequencing that may be coinciding with both the elevated delay and underutilized chair capacity. This could involve a brief template review session with the scheduler and clinical leadership to identify any manual overrides or booking patterns that are diverging from system recommendations, paired with a refresher on iOptimize calibration for the clinic's specific patient mix.

ai_insights.executive_summary: The LOA network posted a composite performance score of 24.0 out of 100 (where 50 represents the network average), reflecting persistent operational headwinds across all four clinics. However, March brought encouraging month-over-month gains: MtPleasant's scheduler compliance rose from 52.6% in February to 64.1% in March, a 21.7% improvement; NorthCharleston's treatments running past closing dropped from 2.8 per day in February to 2.0 in March, a 28.6% reduction; and WestAshely's chair utilization climbed from 60.5% in February to 70.6% in March, a 16.7% gain. These shifts suggest operational traction, though we remain well below the company scheduler compliance average of 68.48%.
highlights: MtPleasant delivered the strongest March performance, with scheduler compliance of 64.1% (up from 52.6% in February) and minutes past close per patient falling dramatically from 45.0 in February to 4.7 in March—a 89.6% reduction that nearly eliminates after-hours treatment spillover. WestAshely showed parallel momentum, with nurse utilization rising from 48.0% in February to 53.0% in March and average patient delay improving from 35.3 minutes in February to 31.9 minutes in March, both moves aligned with a 16.7% lift in chair utilization.
concerns: We noticed WestAshely's average patient delay of 31.9 minutes sits 14% above the company average of 28.01 minutes, the largest performance gap in the network. At the same time, WestAshely's scheduler compliance of 70.2% remains only marginally above company average (68.48%), suggesting scheduling discipline may not yet be tight enough to compress wait times further. We also observed that nurse utilization across all four clinics remains low relative to optimal (Summerville at 44.2%, MtPleasant at 42.1%), a pattern worth exploring together to understand whether staffing deployment or scheduling alignment could improve chair-to-nurse pairing efficiency.
recommendations: We recommend WestAshely conduct a targeted review of its scheduling templates and appointment spacing logic within iOptimize, with focus on how treatment duration estimates map to actual chair turnaround times. Given the clinic's recent gains in chair utilization and nurse staffing, tightening scheduler adherence to iOptimize recommendations—currently at 70.2% versus the company average of 68.48%—may be the lever to narrow the 31.9-minute delay gap and move closer to the 28.0-minute benchmark across the network.

scheduler_compliance.label: Scheduler Compliance
scheduler_compliance.explanation: Percentage of appointments scheduled following iOptimize recommendations. Higher is better. Company average is typically 60-70%. OncoSmart benchmark target is 75%.

avg_delay_mins.label: Avg Delay
avg_delay_mins.explanation: Average daily schedule delay in minutes. Lower is better. Measures how far behind the clinic is running on average.

avg_treatments_per_day.label: Tx Past Close/Day
avg_treatments_per_day.explanation: Average treatments running past treatment close per day (overtime patients per day). Lower is better.

avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.label: Tx Mins Past Close/Patient
avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.explanation: Average treatment minutes past closing time per patient. Lower is better.

avg_chair_utilization.label: Chair Utilization
avg_chair_utilization.explanation: Average chair utilization rate. Higher is better.

iassign_utilization.label: iAssign Utilization
iassign_utilization.explanation: Percentage of nurse assignments completed via iAssign. Higher means more consistent, optimized assignments.

avg_patients_per_nurse.label: Patients/Nurse/Day
avg_patients_per_nurse.explanation: Average patients per nurse per day. Context-dependent -- too high means understaffing, too low means inefficiency.

avg_chairs_per_nurse.label: Chairs/Nurse
avg_chairs_per_nurse.explanation: Average chairs assigned per nurse. Context-dependent.

avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.label: Nurse Utilization
avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.explanation: Average nurse-to-patient in-chair time per day. Higher is better.

chatbot_context.data_notes: Company Avg rows represent the network average across all locations for this client in the given month. Onco rows are global oncology benchmarks from the OncoSmart network. Rows named Global Avg, Network Avg, Total, or Overall are network-wide aggregates, not individual clinic locations. MoM (Month-over-Month) deltas compare the current month to the prior month. Composite scores are weighted 0 to 100 where 100 is best-in-class performance.
